qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Comment serialization allows attacker-controlled XML node injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a comment-boundary XML injection issue, not the advisory's unbounded recursive traversal flaw in `lib/dom.js`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deeply nested XML triggers uncontrolled recursion and parser-time denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory details explicitly include `Node.prototype.normalize()` and describe the same recursion-without-depth-guard root cause in `lib/dom.js`; this finding reaches it through `DOMParser.parseFromString()` -> `DOMHandler.endDocument()` -> `doc.normalize()`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DocumentType systemId serialization allows attacker-controlled markup injection before the root element
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a DOCTYPE serialization injection issue, not the advisory's recursion-driven DoS in `lib/dom.js`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Processing instruction serialization allows attacker-controlled XML node injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a processing-instruction boundary injection issue, not the advisory's unbounded recursive traversal flaw in `lib/dom.js`.

selected_finding.title: Deeply nested XML triggers uncontrolled recursion and parser-time denial of service

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory's shared `lib/dom.js` recursion bug family. Although GHSA-2v35 highlights XML serialization in its summary, the advisory details also name `Node.prototype.normalize()` as an affected traversal with the same missing depth guard, and the copied PoC reproduced the resulting denial-of-service condition on attacker-controlled deep XML.
evidence: The advisory details for GHSA-2v35 explicitly list `Node.prototype.normalize()` among the affected recursive traversals and state that all seven share the same root cause in `lib/dom.js`.

The finding traces attacker-controlled XML through `DOMParser.parseFromString()` into `DOMHandler.endDocument()` and then `doc.normalize()`, which is the advisory-described vulnerable traversal family.

The copied PoC run log at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_38fa38bf/.judge/poc_runs/deep_nesting_dos/run.log` shows `observed error message: Maximum call stack size exceeded` followed by `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: parseFromString() hit RangeError in normalize() with 20000 nested elements`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC cloned upstream tag `0.9.9`, fed 20000 nested elements into `DOMParser.parseFromString()`, logged `Maximum call stack size exceeded`, and only emitted its success marker after confirming the stack trace hit `normalize`.
